label: Red Team Operator
description: Conducts full-scope adversary simulation engagements — C2 infrastructure, EDR evasion, persistence, lateral movement, and objective-based operations simulating real threat actors.

paths.name: Standard Red Team Track
paths.rationale: OSCP provides the foundation; CRTO adds the most respected dedicated red team credential covering Cobalt Strike and adversary simulation; CRTO II (CRTL) adds advanced EDR evasion, WDAC bypass, and modern C2 tradecraft at a fraction of OSEP's cost.
pros: CRTO is the most recognized dedicated red team credential — required by name at many red team roles
CRTO II covers modern EDR evasion, WDAC bypass, and advanced C2 at ~$500 vs OSEP's $1,749
Both Zero-Point Security certs now backed by Fortra — boosting enterprise credibility
cons: Lower HR brand recognition than OSEP for DoD/federal roles — keep OSEP Elite track for those
CRTO II is newer — building market presence vs OSEP's established name
Fortra acquisition may affect Zero-Point pricing/availability long-term

prerequisites.experience: OSCP or equivalent is the minimum entry point.

cert_sequence.notes: Non-negotiable foundation. Network exploitation, AD, and report-writing are the baseline every red team operator needs.

Zero-Point Security red team ops cert — the most respected dedicated red team credential. Cobalt Strike operation, C2 profile customization, OPSEC, multi-stage engagements. Unlimited retakes. 4-day practical exam. With OSCP completed focus on C2 tradecraft and OPSEC.

CRTO II (CRTL) — Zero-Point Security's advanced red team cert. Builds directly on CRTO with modern EDR bypass, WDAC evasion, advanced C2 profile customization, and multi-stage engagement tradecraft. At ~$500 with unlimited retakes it delivers more current evasion content than OSEP at roughly one-third the cost. With CRTO's Cobalt Strike foundation already established, CRTO II focuses your attention on operating against hardened defensive environments.

paths.name: CREST-Aligned Track
paths.rationale: For red team operators targeting UK, Australian, and European markets where CREST accreditation is required for regulated red team engagements.
pros: CREST CCRTS required for regulated red team engagements in UK/AU/EU
Opens government and financial sector red team work
cons: Primarily recognized in UK/AU/EU — limited US recognition
High cost including CREST examination fees

prerequisites.experience: Multiple years hands-on pentesting experience required. CREST expects demonstrated professional experience.

cert_sequence.notes: Foundation credential required before CREST examinations.
C2 and red team tradecraft before the CREST pathway.

CREST Certified Red Team Specialist — team-based adversary simulation, threat intelligence-led testing. Written and practical components. Requires genuine red team engagement experience beyond lab work.

paths.name: OffSec Elite Track
paths.rationale: The pure OffSec prestige ladder. OSCP → OSEP → OSCE³ is the most credentialed offensive career path available. OSCE³ requires passing OSWE, OSED, and OSEP — a combination that proves white-box web exploitation, Windows exploit development, and advanced red team evasion. Held by a very small number of practitioners globally.
pros: OSCE³ is the most prestigious offensive security credential that exists
Each cert proves genuine hands-on skill at increasing difficulty
OSEP specifically validates advanced EDR evasion and red team tradecraft
Career-defining credential set for serious offensive practitioners
cons: OSCE³ requires OSWE + OSED + OSEP — years of preparation each
Extremely high total cost and time investment
Diminishing returns after OSCP + OSEP for most red team roles
OSCE³ is aspirational — most red team leads don't hold it

prerequisites.experience: OSCP and at least 2-3 years active penetration testing experience before OSEP. OSEE / OSED require exploitation research experience.

cert_sequence.notes: CRTO adds the C2 and adversary simulation layer before OSEP's evasion depth. The CRTO Cobalt Strike foundation makes OSEP's C# tooling content more approachable.
OffSec Experienced Penetration Tester — the red team evasion cert. AV/EDR bypass, process injection, living-off-the-land. 48-hour defended environment exam. One of the three certs required for OSCE³.

OffSec Certified Expert 3 — requires passing OSWE (white-box web), OSED (Windows exploit development), and OSEP. Not a single exam — you earn OSCE³ by completing all three. Plan 12+ months for OSED and 6+ months for OSWE after OSEP. This is a multi-year commitment. Most practitioners who attempt it spend 3-5 years on the full progression.
